items.title: Wheat Field with Cypresses
items.blurb: Van Gogh's swirling Provence landscape — one of three closely-related canvases.

items.title: Young Woman with a Water Pitcher
items.blurb: An early-1660s Vermeer — the first work by him to enter an American museum.

items.title: Madame X (Madame Pierre Gautreau)
items.blurb: The portrait that scandalized the 1884 Paris Salon and made Sargent's career.

items.title: Under the Wave off Kanagawa (The Great Wave)
items.blurb: The most reproduced image in art history — Hokusai's towering wave.

items.title: Aristotle with a Bust of Homer
items.blurb: Rembrandt's meditation on knowledge and worldly success — the most expensive painting in the world when the Met bought it in 1961.

items.title: The Harvesters
items.blurb: August panel from Bruegel's revolutionary cycle of the seasons.

items.title: Mäda Primavesi
items.blurb: Klimt's vivid 1912-13 portrait of nine-year-old Mäda Primavesi.

items.title: The Denial of Saint Peter
items.blurb: One of Caravaggio's last paintings — three figures, two pointing fingers.

items.title: Washington Crossing the Delaware
items.blurb: The 12-foot-tall icon of American national mythology, painted in Düsseldorf.

items.title: The Dance Class
items.blurb: Degas at the Paris Opéra — a rehearsal frozen between motion and rest.
